title: Getting Started
content:
### Getting Started with HTML --- ### What You Need | Tool | Purpose | |------|---------| | **Text Editor** | VS Code, Notepad++, Sublime | | **Web Browser** | Chrome, Firefox, Edge | No special software needed -- just a text editor and a browser! --- ### Create Your First HTML File 1. Open a text editor 2. Type the following: ```html

My First Heading

My first paragraph.

``` 3. Save as `index.html` 4. Double-click the file to open in a browser --- ### HTML Editors | Editor | Platform | |--------|----------| | **VS Code** | Free, powerful, popular | | **Sublime Text** | Fast, lightweight | | **Notepad++** | Windows only | | **Online editors** | CodePen, JSFiddle, Replit | --- ### View Page Source Right-click on any web page and select **View Page Source** to see its HTML code. This is a great way to learn! --- ### Developer Tools Press `F12` (or `Ctrl+Shift+I`) to open **Developer Tools**. You can inspect and edit HTML/CSS in real time. > **Pro Tip:** VS Code with the **Live Server** extension automatically refreshes your page when you save changes.

quiz.question: What extension do HTML files use?
